---
title: "AI-Enhanced Curriculum Delivery for Community Health Workers"
subtitle: "Evidence and Design from the Healthy Future Platform"
author: "Sean Sylvia · Gary Darmstadt · Yunwei Chen · Ann Weber · Siva Balakrishnan"
date: "March 28, 2026"
date-format: long
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, ../style/hf.scss]
    slide-number: true
    transition: fade
    progress: true
    incremental: false
    center: false
    scrollable: false
    smaller: false
    footer: "Sylvia et al. | Stanford REAP Conference, Leuven 2026"
    toc: false
    chalkboard: false
    preview-links: auto
    width: 1280
    height: 720
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r setup}
library(haven)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
library(forcats)
library(tibble)
library(stringr)
library(fixest)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(glue)

df <- read_dta(here::here("data", "HF_main_anasam.dta"))

HF_BLUE   <- "#1f4e79"
HF_TEAL   <- "#2e75b6"
HF_ORANGE <- "#ed7d31"
HF_GREEN  <- "#4a7c59"
HF_GRAY   <- "#767171"
HF_LIGHTBG <- "#f7f9fc"

lasso_cols <- df |> select(starts_with(c("_CHILD_","_FAM_","_CARE_","_CID_"))) |> names()

add_stars <- function(p) case_when(p<0.01~"***", p<0.05~"**", p<0.10~"*", TRUE~"")
bt <- function(x) paste0("`", x, "`")

run_itt <- function(outcome, data = df) {
  controls <- lasso_cols[lasso_cols %in% names(data)]
  rhs <- paste(c("treat", bt(controls)), collapse = " + ")
  fml <- as.formula(paste(bt(outcome), "~", rhs))
  feols(fml, data = data, cluster = ~TID, warn = FALSE)
}
```

---

## {.center background-color="#1f4e79"}

::: {style="color: white; text-align: center; padding-top: 60px;"}

<div style="font-size: 1.6em; font-weight: 700; line-height: 1.3; margin-bottom: 0.6em;">
AI-Enhanced Curriculum Delivery<br>for Community Health Workers
</div>

<div style="font-size: 1.1em; color: #b8d4f0; margin-bottom: 1.2em;">
Evidence and Design from the Healthy Future Platform
</div>

<div style="font-size: 0.85em; color: #cce0f5; margin-bottom: 1.5em;">
Sean Sylvia (UNC Chapel Hill) &nbsp;·&nbsp; Gary Darmstadt & Yunwei Chen (Stanford)<br>
Ann Weber & Siva Balakrishnan (University of Nevada, Reno)
</div>

<div style="font-size: 0.8em; color: #9ec5e8; border-top: 1px solid #3a7ab8; padding-top: 0.8em;">
Stanford REAP Conference &nbsp;·&nbsp; Irish College Leuven &nbsp;·&nbsp; March 28, 2026
</div>

:::

---

## The Problem: One Visit, Many Needs

::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}

<div style="font-size: 1.05em; font-weight: 600; color: #1f4e79; margin-bottom: 0.8em;">A CHW has ~30 minutes per home visit</div>

<div class="visual-box"><span class="icon">👶</span><div><strong>Breastfeeding</strong> — initiation, exclusivity, timing</div></div>
<div class="visual-box"><span class="icon">🥗</span><div><strong>Complementary feeding</strong> — diversity, frequency</div></div>
<div class="visual-box"><span class="icon">🧠</span><div><strong>Maternal mental health</strong> — depression, anxiety</div></div>
<div class="visual-box"><span class="icon">🏥</span><div><strong>Health service uptake</strong> — vaccines, checkups</div></div>
<div class="visual-box"><span class="icon">🤲</span><div><strong>Daily care & stimulation</strong> — responsive caregiving</div></div>

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

<div style="text-align: center; padding: 1.5em 1em;">
<div style="font-size: 5em;">⏱️</div>
<div style="font-size: 1.8em; font-weight: 700; color: #1f4e79; margin: 0.3em 0;">30 minutes</div>
<div style="color: #767171; font-size: 0.95em;">per visit · dozens of families · hundreds of content decisions</div>
</div>

::: {.key-finding}
**Standard approach:** all families at the same developmental stage get the **same content** — regardless of circumstances.
:::

:::
:::

---

## The Healthy Future Program

::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}

<div style="font-size: 1.05em; font-weight: 600; color: #1f4e79; margin-bottom: 0.8em;">CareLoom — the app behind the CHW visit</div>

<div class="visual-box"><span class="icon">📱</span><div><strong>App-guided visits</strong> — CHW follows structured curriculum, app handles the decisions</div></div>
<div class="visual-box"><span class="icon">🗓️</span><div><strong>Stage-based delivery</strong> — new content each 3-month stage, then reinforcement</div></div>
<div class="visual-box"><span class="icon">📶</span><div><strong>Offline-first</strong> — works in rural, low-connectivity settings</div></div>
<div class="visual-box"><span class="icon">🔓</span><div><strong>Open-source MIT</strong> — github.com/DHEPLab</div></div>

<div style="margin-top: 0.6em; font-size: 0.85em;">
[**See the app in action →**](https://www.canva.com/design/DAG_D0qj1Ec/jv1UuoK_JNEK8PZM6CMg0g/view){target="_blank" style="color: #2e75b6; font-weight: 600;"}
</div>

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

<div style="text-align: center; padding: 0.5em 0; margin-bottom: 0.8em;">

```{r hf-flow}
#| fig-height: 3.8
#| fig-width: 4.8
library(ggplot2)

boxes <- tibble(
  x = c(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5),
  y = c(3.5, 2.5, 1.5, 0.5),
  label = c("Pregnancy", "0–6 months", "6–12 months", "12–18 months"),
  fill = c("#dce6f1","#dce6f1","#e6f3ec","#e6f3ec")
)

arrows <- tibble(x=0.5, xend=0.5, y=c(3.2,2.2,1.2), yend=c(2.8,1.8,0.8))

ggplot() +
  geom_tile(data=boxes, aes(x=x, y=y, fill=fill), width=0.85, height=0.5, color="#2e75b6", linewidth=0.8) +
  geom_text(data=boxes, aes(x=x, y=y, label=label), fontface="bold", size=4.2, color="#1f4e79") +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  geom_segment(data=arrows, aes(x=x, xend=xend, y=y, yend=yend),
               arrow=arrow(length=unit(0.12,"inches"), type="closed"),
               color="#2e75b6", linewidth=1) +
  annotate("text", x=0.5, y=4.15, label="CHW Home Visits", fontface="bold",
           size=4.5, color="#1f4e79") +
  annotate("text", x=0.5, y=0.0, label="Stage-based · App-guided · Monthly",
           size=3.2, color="#767171") +
  xlim(0, 1) + ylim(-0.3, 4.5) +
  theme_void()
```

</div>

::: {.policy}
The app removes the burden — CHWs don't track where each family is or decide what to teach. The platform does.
:::

:::
:::

---

## Study Design

::: columns
::: {.column width="48%"}

<div style="font-size: 1.05em; font-weight: 600; color: #1f4e79; margin-bottom: 0.8em;">Cluster RCT — Rural China</div>

<div style="display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 0.6em; margin-bottom: 0.8em;">
<div style="background:#f7f9fc; border-radius:8px; padding:0.8em; text-align:center;">
<span class="big-number">119</span>
<span class="big-number-label">townships randomized<br>(40 treat / 79 control)</span>
</div>
<div style="background:#f7f9fc; border-radius:8px; padding:0.8em; text-align:center;">
<span class="big-number">1,306</span>
<span class="big-number-label">families enrolled<br>across 4 counties</span>
</div>
<div style="background:#f7f9fc; border-radius:8px; padding:0.8em; text-align:center;">
<span class="big-number">12</span>
<span class="big-number-label">months of CHW<br>home visits</span>
</div>
<div style="background:#f7f9fc; border-radius:8px; padding:0.8em; text-align:center;">
<span class="big-number">88%</span>
<span class="big-number-label">follow-up rate<br>(COVID-period)</span>
</div>
</div>

<div style="font-size: 0.82em; color: #767171;">
499 pregnant women enrolled · 807 families with infants &lt;6m<br>
Post-double-selection LASSO · County FEs · Cluster SEs (township)
</div>

:::
::: {.column width="52%"}

```{r baseline-chart}
#| fig-height: 4.2
#| fig-width: 5.5

stats <- tibble(
  label = c("Exclusive BF\nat baseline", "Any mental\nhealth concern", "Maternal\ndepression", "Maternal\nanxiety"),
  pct   = c(0.32, 0.43, 0.19, 0.21),
  color = c(HF_TEAL, HF_ORANGE, HF_ORANGE, HF_ORANGE)
)

ggplot(stats, aes(x = reorder(label, pct), y = pct, fill = color)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::percent(pct, accuracy = 1)),
            hjust = -0.2, size = 5, fontface = "bold", color = "#2c2c2c") +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 0.62), labels = scales::percent) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Baseline Profile", x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = HF_BLUE, size = 15),
        panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 13))
```

:::
:::

---

## What CHWs Deliver: 12 Modules Across Development Stages

![Healthy Future curriculum: 12 content modules delivered from pregnancy through 18 months, covering nutrition, breastfeeding, preventive care, and mental health](../images/HF-Fig1.png){fig-alt="Timeline showing 12 intervention modules from pregnancy through 18 months" width="95%"}

::: columns
::: {.column width="55%"}

::: {.key-finding style="font-size: 0.9em;"}
**Completion:** 82% of treated families participated. On average, families received **10.5 home visits** and completed modules across breastfeeding (7.5), complementary feeding (6.7), preventive care (6.5), and mental health (5.2).
:::

:::
::: {.column width="45%"}

![](../images/HF-Table2.png){width="100%"}

:::
:::

---

## Primary Outcomes {.center}

```{r primary-compute}
primary_vars <- c(
  "FLVAR_CH_HEMO"  = "Hemoglobin Level",
  "FLVAR_FEED_EBF" = "Exclusive Breastfeeding",
  "FLVAR_FEED_DDS" = "Dietary Diversity Score"
)

primary_res <- map_dfr(names(primary_vars), function(v) {
  m <- run_itt(v)
  tibble(
    outcome  = primary_vars[[v]],
    b        = coef(m)["treat"],
    se       = fixest::se(m)["treat"],
    p        = fixest::pvalue(m)["treat"],
    ci_lo    = coef(m)["treat"] - 1.645 * fixest::se(m)["treat"],
    ci_hi    = coef(m)["treat"] + 1.645 * fixest::se(m)["treat"]
  )
}) |> mutate(sig = p < 0.10)
```

<div style="display: flex; gap: 2em; justify-content: center; align-items: stretch; margin-top: 1.5em;">

<div style="flex: 1; max-width: 300px;">
<div class="outcome-label">Hemoglobin Level</div>
<div class="outcome-box outcome-null">
<div class="outcome-effect">No effect</div>
<div class="outcome-sub">β = `r round(primary_res$b[1],3)` · p = `r round(primary_res$p[1],2)`</div>
</div>
<div style="font-size:0.8em; color:#767171; text-align:center; margin-top:0.5em;">Biomarker outcomes need<br>longer time horizons</div>
</div>

<div style="flex: 1; max-width: 300px;">
<div class="outcome-label">Exclusive Breastfeeding</div>
<div class="outcome-box outcome-null">
<div class="outcome-effect">No effect</div>
<div class="outcome-sub">β = `r round(primary_res$b[2],3)` · p = `r round(primary_res$p[2],2)`</div>
</div>
<div style="font-size:0.8em; color:#767171; text-align:center; margin-top:0.5em;">Baseline rates already<br>relatively high</div>
</div>

<div style="flex: 1; max-width: 300px;">
<div class="outcome-label">Dietary Diversity Score</div>
<div class="outcome-box outcome-positive">
<div class="outcome-effect">↑ Significant</div>
<div class="outcome-sub">β = `r round(primary_res$b[3],3)``r add_stars(primary_res$p[3])` · p = `r round(primary_res$p[3],2)`</div>
</div>
<div style="font-size:0.8em; color:#767171; text-align:center; margin-top:0.5em;">Proximate pathway —<br>where 12-month programs work first</div>
</div>

</div>

::: {.policy style="margin-top: 1.5em; font-size: 0.95em;"}
**Why dietary diversity?** Feeding practice changes are the proximate pathway. Biomarker and growth effects require longer follow-up — this is expected, not a failure.
:::

---

## Secondary Outcomes: Where We See the Impact

```{r sec-compute}
domain_vars <- tribble(
  ~var,            ~label,                          ~group,
  "IN_CH",          "Child Health Index",             "child",
  "IN_BFI",         "Breastfeeding Initiation",       "child",
  "IN_CF",          "Complementary Feeding",           "child",
  "IN_PRIKNOW_CH",  "Caregiving Knowledge",            "caregiver",
  "IN_EPDS",        "Maternal Mental Health (EPDS)",   "caregiver",
  "IN_DASS",        "Caregiver Mental Health (DASS)",  "caregiver",
  "IN_SS",          "Perceived Social Support",        "caregiver"
)

sec_res <- map_dfr(domain_vars$var, function(v) {
  m <- run_itt(v)
  tibble(var=v, b=coef(m)["treat"], se=fixest::se(m)["treat"],
         p=fixest::pvalue(m)["treat"],
         ci_lo=coef(m)["treat"] - 1.645 * fixest::se(m)["treat"],
         ci_hi=coef(m)["treat"] + 1.645 * fixest::se(m)["treat"])
}) |> left_join(domain_vars, by="var") |>
  mutate(label=factor(label, levels=rev(domain_vars$label)),
         sig=p<0.10,
         coeff_label=glue("{round(b,3)}{add_stars(p)}"))
```

```{r sec-grouped-bar}
#| fig-height: 4.4
#| fig-width: 11

sec_plot <- sec_res |>
  mutate(
    group_label = ifelse(group=="child","Child Outcomes","Caregiver Outcomes"),
    fill_col = case_when(sig & group=="child" ~ HF_TEAL,
                          sig & group=="caregiver" ~ HF_ORANGE,
                          TRUE ~ "#d0d0d0"),
    label_short = c("Child\nHealth\nIndex","Breastfeeding\nInitiation","Comp.\nFeeding",
                    "Caregiving\nKnowledge","Maternal MH\n(EPDS)","Caregiver MH\n(DASS)","Social\nSupport")
  )

ggplot(sec_plot, aes(x=label_short, y=b, fill=fill_col)) +
  geom_col(width=0.65) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=ci_lo, ymax=ci_hi), width=0.2, linewidth=0.9, color="#555") +
  geom_hline(yintercept=0, linewidth=0.8, color="#333") +
  geom_text(aes(label=coeff_label,
                y=ifelse(b>=0, ci_hi+0.03, ci_lo-0.03),
                vjust=ifelse(b>=0, 0, 1)),
            size=3.8, fontface="bold", color="#333") +
  geom_vline(xintercept=3.5, linetype="dashed", color="#aaa", linewidth=0.8) +
  annotate("text", x=2, y=max(sec_plot$ci_hi, na.rm=TRUE)*1.15,
           label="Child Outcomes", fontface="bold", color=HF_BLUE, size=4.2) +
  annotate("text", x=5.5, y=max(sec_plot$ci_hi, na.rm=TRUE)*1.15,
           label="Caregiver Outcomes", fontface="bold", color=HF_ORANGE, size=4.2) +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  scale_y_continuous(expand=expansion(mult=c(0.15,0.2))) +
  labs(x=NULL, y="Treatment Effect (SD units, 90% CI)",
       caption="Color = significant (p<0.10) · Grey = not significant · OLS · LASSO controls + county FEs") +
  theme_minimal(base_size=13) +
  theme(panel.grid.major.x=element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor=element_blank(),
        plot.caption=element_text(color=HF_GRAY, size=9),
        axis.text.x=element_text(size=11, lineheight=1.1))
```

---

## When Does It Work Best? Entry Timing

```{r timing-compute}
df2 <- df |>
  mutate(
    age_entry = BBVAR_AGE_MON_BASE2,
    entry_bin = case_when(
      treat == 0                        ~ "control",
      age_entry < -3                    ~ "preg_early",
      age_entry >= -3 & age_entry < 0   ~ "preg_late",
      age_entry >= 0  & age_entry < 3   ~ "inf_0_3",
      age_entry >= 3  & age_entry < 6   ~ "inf_3_6",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

bins_order  <- c("preg_early","preg_late","inf_0_3","inf_3_6")
bin_labels  <- c("Pregnancy\n(early)","Pregnancy\n(late)","Infancy\n0–3m","Infancy\n3–6m")
bin_shading <- c("pregnancy","pregnancy","infancy","infancy")

event_vars <- tribble(
  ~var,            ~title,
  "IN_CH",          "(a) Child Health Index",
  "IN_DASS",        "(b) Caregiver Mental Health",
  "IN_PRIKNOW_CH",  "(c) Caregiving Knowledge",
  "IN_SS",          "(d) Social Support"
)

event_res <- map_dfr(event_vars$var, function(v) {
  map_dfr(bins_order, function(b) {
    sub <- df2 |> filter(entry_bin==b | treat==0) |>
      mutate(d=as.integer(entry_bin==b & treat==1))
    ctrl_here <- lasso_cols[lasso_cols %in% names(sub)]
    fml <- as.formula(paste(bt(v),"~ d +",paste(bt(ctrl_here),collapse=" + ")))
    m   <- feols(fml, data=sub, cluster=~TID, warn=FALSE)
    tibble(var=v, bin=b, b=coef(m)["d"], se=fixest::se(m)["d"],
           p=fixest::pvalue(m)["d"],
           ci_lo=coef(m)["d"] - 1.645 * fixest::se(m)["d"],
           ci_hi=coef(m)["d"] + 1.645 * fixest::se(m)["d"])
  })
}) |>
  mutate(bin=factor(bin,levels=bins_order,labels=bin_labels),
         shading=rep(rep(bin_shading,length(event_vars$var)),1),
         sig=p<0.10) |>
  left_join(event_vars, by="var") |>
  mutate(title=factor(title,levels=event_vars$title))
```

```{r timing-plot}
#| fig-height: 4.2
#| fig-width: 11

ggplot(event_res, aes(x=bin, y=b, fill=sig)) +
  annotate("rect", xmin=0.5, xmax=2.5, ymin=-Inf, ymax=Inf, fill="#dce6f1", alpha=0.4) +
  annotate("rect", xmin=2.5, xmax=4.5, ymin=-Inf, ymax=Inf, fill="#e6f3ec", alpha=0.3) +
  geom_hline(yintercept=0, linewidth=0.9, color="#333") +
  geom_col(aes(fill=sig), width=0.6) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=ci_lo, ymax=ci_hi), width=0.18, linewidth=0.9, color="#444") +
  geom_vline(xintercept=2.5, linetype="dashed", color="#999", linewidth=0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("FALSE"="#cccccc","TRUE"=HF_TEAL), guide="none") +
  facet_wrap(~title, ncol=4) +
  labs(x="Stage at first exposure", y="Effect size (SD, 90% CI)",
       caption="Blue shading = pregnancy entry · Green shading = infancy entry · Filled = p<0.10") +
  theme_minimal(base_size=13) +
  theme(strip.text=element_text(face="bold", size=12, color=HF_BLUE),
        panel.grid.major.x=element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor=element_blank(),
        plot.caption=element_text(color=HF_GRAY, size=9),
        axis.text.x=element_text(size=10, lineheight=1.1))
```

::: {.policy style="font-size: 0.95em;"}
**Key finding:** Pregnancy enrollment consistently delivers the strongest gains. Programs that start CHW engagement only post-partum are missing the highest-return window.
:::

---

## Short-Term Signals Predict Long-Run Health

```{r surr-compute}
ctrl_df <- df |> filter(treat==0)
z_score <- function(x) (x-mean(x,na.rm=TRUE))/sd(x,na.rm=TRUE)

surrogates <- tribble(
  ~var,            ~label,
  "IN_CF",          "Complementary Feeding",
  "IN_BFI",         "Breastfeeding Initiation",
  "IN_PRIKNOW_CH",  "Caregiving Knowledge",
  "IN_DASS",        "Caregiver Mental Health",
  "IN_SS",          "Perceived Social Support"
)

ctrl_std <- ctrl_df |>
  mutate(across(c(all_of(surrogates$var),"IN_CH"), z_score, .names="z_{.col}"))

ctrl_county <- lasso_cols[lasso_cols %in% names(ctrl_std)]

surr_res <- map_dfr(surrogates$var, function(v) {
  fml <- as.formula(paste(bt("z_IN_CH"), "~", bt(paste0("z_", v)), "+", paste(bt(ctrl_county), collapse = " + ")))
  m   <- feols(fml, data=ctrl_std, cluster=~TID, warn=FALSE)
  cn  <- paste0("z_",v)
  tibble(var=v, b=coef(m)[cn], se=fixest::se(m)[cn], p=fixest::pvalue(m)[cn],
         ci_lo=coef(m)[cn] - 1.96 * fixest::se(m)[cn],
         ci_hi=coef(m)[cn] + 1.96 * fixest::se(m)[cn], n=nobs(m))
}) |> left_join(surrogates, by="var") |>
  mutate(label=factor(label,levels=rev(surrogates$label)), sig=p<0.10,
         coeff_label=glue("{round(b,3)}{add_stars(p)}"))
```

::: columns
::: {.column width="55%"}

```{r surr-plot}
#| fig-height: 3.8
#| fig-width: 6.5

ggplot(surr_res, aes(y=label, x=b, xmin=ci_lo, xmax=ci_hi, color=sig)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept=0, linetype="dashed", color=HF_GRAY, linewidth=0.7) +
  geom_errorbarh(height=0.25, linewidth=1.3) +
  geom_point(aes(shape=sig), size=5.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label=coeff_label, x=ci_hi), hjust=-0.15, size=4.2, fontface="bold") +
  scale_color_manual(values=c("FALSE"=HF_GRAY,"TRUE"=HF_ORANGE), guide="none") +
  scale_shape_manual(values=c("FALSE"=1,"TRUE"=19), guide="none") +
  scale_x_continuous(expand=expansion(mult=c(0.05,0.35))) +
  labs(x="Association with Child Health Index (SD units)", y=NULL,
       subtitle="Control group only · 95% CI",
       caption="Standardized OLS · County FEs · Cluster SEs by township") +
  theme_minimal(base_size=14) +
  theme(panel.grid.minor=element_blank(),
        plot.subtitle=element_text(color=HF_GRAY, size=12),
        plot.caption=element_text(color=HF_GRAY, size=9),
        axis.text=element_text(size=12))
```

:::
::: {.column width="45%"}

<div style="font-size: 0.95em; margin-bottom: 0.8em; color: #1f4e79; font-weight: 600;">What this shows</div>

In the **control group**, families with better follow-up values on these intermediate outcomes are on stronger long-run child health trajectories.

**Implication:** short-term gains are **leading indicators** — not just outputs.

::: {.key-finding style="margin-top: 0.8em;"}
**For HF 2.0:** Tracking these signals in real time creates a feedback loop for content adaptation — without waiting 12–24 months for child health outcomes.

Foundation for the **surrogate index framework** (Athey et al. 2019).
:::

:::
:::

---

## HF 1.0 → HF 2.0: The Evolution

::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}

```{r evolution-diagram}
#| fig-height: 4.5
#| fig-width: 5.5

boxes <- tibble(
  x    = c(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5),
  y    = c(3.5, 2.5, 1.5, 0.5),
  fill = c("#dce6f1","#dce6f1","#e6f3ec","#e6f3ec"),
  border= c("#2e75b6","#2e75b6","#4a7c59","#4a7c59"),
  lbl  = c("HF 1.0 — Stage-based","","HF 2.0 — Circumstances-based","")
)

hf1 <- tibble(
  x    = c(0.25, 0.75),
  y    = c(3.5, 3.5),
  label= c("Stage X", "Same content\nfor all families"),
  fill = c("#dce6f1","#dce6f1")
)

hf2_inputs <- tibble(
  x    = c(0.18, 0.5, 0.82),
  y    = c(1.8, 1.8, 1.8),
  label= c("Family\ncharacteristics","Response\nsignals","CHW\njudgment"),
  fill = c("#e6f3ec","#e6f3ec","#e6f3ec")
)

hf2_output <- tibble(x=0.5, y=0.7, label="Tailored content\npriority", fill="#4a7c59")

ggplot() +
  # HF 1.0 section
  annotate("rect", xmin=0, xmax=1, ymin=3.1, ymax=3.9, fill="#dce6f1", alpha=0.5, color="#2e75b6", linewidth=0.8) +
  annotate("text", x=0.08, y=3.85, label="HF 1.0", fontface="bold", color="#1f4e79", size=4, hjust=0) +
  geom_tile(data=hf1, aes(x=x, y=y, fill=fill), width=0.42, height=0.45, color="#2e75b6", linewidth=0.7) +
  geom_text(data=hf1, aes(x=x, y=y, label=label), size=3.5, color="#1f4e79", fontface="bold", lineheight=1.1) +
  annotate("segment", x=0.46, xend=0.54, y=3.5, yend=3.5,
           arrow=arrow(length=unit(0.1,"in"),type="closed"), color="#2e75b6", linewidth=1) +
  # Arrow down
  annotate("segment", x=0.5, xend=0.5, y=3.1, yend=2.7,
           arrow=arrow(length=unit(0.1,"in"),type="closed"), color="#888", linewidth=0.8, linetype="dashed") +
  annotate("text", x=0.5, y=2.9, label="HF 2.0", fontface="bold", color="#4a7c59", size=4) +
  # HF 2.0 section
  annotate("rect", xmin=0, xmax=1, ymin=0.3, ymax=2.5, fill="#e6f3ec", alpha=0.4, color="#4a7c59", linewidth=0.8) +
  geom_tile(data=hf2_inputs, aes(x=x, y=y, fill=fill), width=0.29, height=0.45, color="#4a7c59", linewidth=0.7) +
  geom_text(data=hf2_inputs, aes(x=x, y=y, label=label), size=3.2, color="#2c5f3a", fontface="bold", lineheight=1.1) +
  # Arrows converging
  annotate("segment", x=0.18, xend=0.43, y=1.57, yend=1.0, arrow=arrow(length=unit(0.09,"in"),type="closed"), color="#4a7c59", linewidth=0.8) +
  annotate("segment", x=0.5,  xend=0.5,  y=1.57, yend=1.0, arrow=arrow(length=unit(0.09,"in"),type="closed"), color="#4a7c59", linewidth=0.8) +
  annotate("segment", x=0.82, xend=0.57, y=1.57, yend=1.0, arrow=arrow(length=unit(0.09,"in"),type="closed"), color="#4a7c59", linewidth=0.8) +
  geom_tile(data=hf2_output, aes(x=x, y=y, fill=fill), width=0.55, height=0.45, color="#2c5f3a", linewidth=1) +
  geom_text(data=hf2_output, aes(x=x, y=y, label=label), size=3.8, color="white", fontface="bold", lineheight=1.1) +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  xlim(0, 1) + ylim(0.1, 4.1) +
  theme_void()
```

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

<div style="font-size: 1.0em; font-weight: 600; color: #1f4e79; margin-bottom: 0.7em;">The gap HF 2.0 closes</div>

::: {.key-finding}
**Same stage ≠ same family**

Two families with a 3-month-old infant may have completely different needs — breastfeeding history, maternal depression, knowledge gaps — but HF 1.0 delivers identical content.
:::

<div class="visual-box" style="margin-top: 0.8em;"><span class="icon">🤖</span><div><strong>AI-based prioritization</strong> — rank content relevance from family data + response patterns</div></div>
<div class="visual-box"><span class="icon">🗣️</span><div><strong>Preference elicitation</strong> — structured family choice bypasses cold-start data problem</div></div>

::: {.policy style="margin-top: 0.8em;"}
**Funded:** Cyrus Tang Foundation · 3-year program · Starting 2025<br>
**Built on:** CareLoom (open-source) · github.com/DHEPLab
:::

:::
:::

---

## The Personalization Challenge in LMIC Settings

::: columns
::: {.column width="52%"}

<div style="font-size: 1.05em; font-weight: 600; color: #1f4e79; margin-bottom: 0.8em;">The challenge isn't the algorithm — it's the signal</div>

```{r challenge-table}
#| fig-height: 3.2
#| fig-width: 5.8

# Use numeric y positions for a fully continuous coordinate system
tbl_pos <- tibble(
  y_pos    = c(2, 1),
  setting  = c("Online platform", "HF CHW program"),
  fill_col = c("#dce6f1","#fff3e0"),
  Users    = c("Millions", "~1,000s"),
  Signal   = c("Clicks,\nseconds", "Outcomes,\nmonths"),
  Data     = c("Continuous", "30 min/visit"),
  Learning = c("Fast", "Slow")
)

ggplot(tbl_pos) +
  # background tiles
  geom_rect(aes(xmin=-0.2, xmax=8.2, ymin=y_pos-0.4, ymax=y_pos+0.4, fill=fill_col), alpha=0.6) +
  # header row
  annotate("text", x=c(0.8,2.8,4.8,6.8), y=2.85,
           label=c("Scale","Feedback signal","Data per family","Learning speed"),
           fontface="bold", color=HF_GRAY, size=3.8, hjust=0.5) +
  annotate("segment", x=-0.2, xend=8.2, y=2.55, yend=2.55, color="#ccc", linewidth=0.6) +
  # setting names
  geom_text(data=tbl_pos, aes(x=-0.1, y=y_pos, label=setting), hjust=1,
            size=3.8, fontface="bold", color=HF_BLUE) +
  # data cells
  geom_text(data=tbl_pos, aes(x=0.8, y=y_pos, label=Users),    size=3.8, hjust=0.5) +
  geom_text(data=tbl_pos, aes(x=2.8, y=y_pos, label=Signal),   size=3.8, hjust=0.5, lineheight=1.0) +
  geom_text(data=tbl_pos, aes(x=4.8, y=y_pos, label=Data),     size=3.8, hjust=0.5) +
  geom_text(data=tbl_pos, aes(x=6.8, y=y_pos, label=Learning), size=3.8, hjust=0.5) +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(-2.5, 8.5), ylim=c(0.4, 3.2)) +
  theme_void()
```

:::
::: {.column width="48%"}

<div style="font-size: 1.0em; font-weight: 600; color: #1f4e79; margin-bottom: 0.5em;">Two solutions</div>

::: {.key-finding}
**Surrogate index**

Short-term intermediate outcomes that predict long-run child health → generate usable optimization signal faster — without waiting years
:::

::: {.policy style="margin-top: 0.8em;"}
**Preference elicitation**

Families have private knowledge about their own circumstances → structured choice bypasses the cold-start data problem entirely
:::

:::
:::

---

## Implications for Scale

::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}

<div style="font-size: 1.05em; font-weight: 600; color: #1f4e79; margin-bottom: 0.8em;">Three actionable levers</div>

<div class="visual-box"><span class="icon">📅</span><div><strong>When</strong> — Enroll during pregnancy, not just post-partum</div></div>
<div class="visual-box"><span class="icon">🎯</span><div><strong>What</strong> — Personalize by family circumstances, not just stage</div></div>
<div class="visual-box"><span class="icon">📈</span><div><strong>How</strong> — Use short-term signals to learn faster and adapt</div></div>

<div style="margin-top: 1em; font-size: 0.9em; color: #767171;">
China has ~1 million CHWs · Small per-family gains compound at national scale<br>
CareLoom is open-source — any MCH program can adapt it
</div>

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

<div style="font-size: 1.0em; font-weight: 600; color: #1f4e79; margin-bottom: 0.5em;">For national program design</div>

::: {.policy}
The entry-timing finding is the most immediately actionable result:

**Pregnancy enrollment is a priority design choice.**

Programs that begin CHW engagement only post-partum are systematically missing the highest-return window — and the data now shows by how much.
:::

::: {.key-finding style="margin-top: 0.8em;"}
HF 2.0 builds the next layer: moving from knowing *when* to start, to knowing *what* to deliver and *to whom* — at scale.
:::

:::
:::

---

## Summary {.center}

<div style="display: flex; gap: 2.5em; justify-content: center; margin-top: 1em; margin-bottom: 1.5em;">

<div style="flex:1; max-width: 300px; text-align: center; padding: 0.5em;">
<div class="takeaway-num">1</div>
<div class="takeaway-title">The program works</div>
<div class="takeaway-text">Significant effects on dietary diversity, feeding practices, maternal mental health, and caregiving knowledge — delivered through CHW home visits via CareLoom.</div>
</div>

<div style="flex:1; max-width: 300px; text-align: center; padding: 0.5em;">
<div class="takeaway-num">2</div>
<div class="takeaway-title">Early entry matters</div>
<div class="takeaway-text">Families enrolled during pregnancy see the strongest child health gains. Intermediate outcomes at follow-up predict long-run trajectories.</div>
</div>

<div style="flex:1; max-width: 300px; text-align: center; padding: 0.5em;">
<div class="takeaway-num">3</div>
<div class="takeaway-title">The next frontier</div>
<div class="takeaway-text">Moving from stage-based to circumstances-based personalization. HF 2.0 is a funded 3-year program — built on open-source CareLoom.</div>
</div>

</div>

::: {.key-finding style="text-align: center; font-size: 1.0em; max-width: 700px; margin: 0 auto;"}
*When does algorithmic content tailoring outperform asking families directly what they need? HF 2.0 is designed to answer that question.*
:::

---

## {.center background-color="#1f4e79"}

::: {style="color: white; text-align: center; padding-top: 60px;"}

<div style="font-size: 1.5em; font-weight: 700; margin-bottom: 0.6em;">Thank You</div>

<div style="font-size: 1.0em; color: #cce0f5; margin-bottom: 0.4em;">
<strong style="color:white;">Sean Sylvia</strong> | sysylvia@email.unc.edu<br>
UNC Gillings School of Global Public Health
</div>

<div style="font-size: 0.85em; color: #9ec5e8; border-top: 1px solid #3a7ab8; padding-top: 0.8em; margin-top: 1em;">
Cyrus Tang Foundation · Stanford REAP · UNC Gillings · University of Nevada, Reno<br>
Sichuan University · The CHWs and families who participated
</div>

<div style="margin-top: 1em; font-size: 0.9em; color: #b8d4f0;">
<strong>CareLoom platform:</strong> github.com/DHEPLab &nbsp;·&nbsp;
<strong>Slides & analysis:</strong> github.com/sysylvia/hf-belgium-talk
</div>

:::

---

# Appendix {.center background-color="#595959"}

---

## A1 — Post-Double-Selection LASSO

**Why this method?**

- Standard RCT regression: add pre-specified controls; risk of omitting important predictors
- PDS-LASSO runs two separate LASSO procedures (on outcome and treatment) to identify which baseline covariates are strong predictors; includes those in the final regression
- Result: improved statistical power, reduced SEs, without arbitrary variable selection
- In HF: baseline maternal dietary diversity was selected → controlling for it substantially reduced SEs on the DDS outcome (p: 0.051 → 0.035)
- Increasingly standard in economics RCT papers; still novel in public health literature

**Pre-generated controls in `HF_main_anasam.dta`:**  
`_CHILD_*`, `_FAM_*`, `_CARE_*`, `_CID_*` — already constructed by the Stata pipeline; used directly in this R analysis.

---

## A2 — Surrogate Index Framework

**Technical details (Athey et al. 2019)**

Construct a composite $S$ of short-run outcomes that together predict long-run outcome $Y$ in the control group:

$$E[Y \mid X, T] \approx E[E[Y \mid S, X] \mid X, T]$$

**In HF context — candidate surrogates:**
- Dietary diversity score (6-month interim)
- Breastfeeding practices
- Caregiver knowledge index
- Maternal depression score
- Perceived social support

**HF 2.0 Phase 1:** Formally estimate surrogate index; validate on HF 1.0 control group data; use as optimization target for content recommendation algorithm.

---

## A3 — Heterogeneous Effects: Causal Forest Analysis

```{r cate-appendix}
#| fig-height: 3.8
#| fig-width: 10

library(grf)

# BLVAR_FEED_EBF excluded (39% structurally missing — pregnancy enrollees)
cate_vars <- c(
  "BBVAR_AGE_MON", "BBVAR_BOY",
  "HHVAR_MOM_AGE_BASE", "HHVAR_MOM_HIEDU", "HHVAR_ASSET",
  "HHVAR_SIB", "HHVAR_SIZE_BASE", "HHVAR_MOM_MIGHIS", "HHVAR_MOM_HOMETOWN",
  "BLVAR_DASSPRI_DEP", "BLVAR_DASSPRI_ANX", "BLVAR_DASSPRI_STR",
  "BLVAR_KNOWPRI_INX", "BLVAR_SSPRI_ALL", "BLVAR_MFEED_DDS"
)

cf_df <- df |> select(all_of(c("FLVAR_FEED_DDS","treat","TID",cate_vars))) |> tidyr::drop_na()
Y <- as.numeric(cf_df$FLVAR_FEED_DDS)
W <- as.numeric(cf_df$treat)
X <- cf_df |> select(all_of(cate_vars)) |> as.matrix()
clusters <- as.integer(as.factor(cf_df$TID))

set.seed(2026)
cf <- causal_forest(X, Y, W, clusters=clusters, num.trees=2000, seed=2026)
ate <- average_treatment_effect(cf)
blp <- test_calibration(cf)
blp_mat <- as.matrix(blp)
blp_p <- blp_mat["differential.forest.prediction", ncol(blp_mat)]

cates <- predict(cf)$predictions
ate_est <- ate[["estimate"]]

p1 <- ggplot(tibble(cate=cates), aes(x=cate)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth=0.05, fill=HF_TEAL, color="white", alpha=0.85) +
  geom_vline(xintercept=ate_est, color=HF_ORANGE, linewidth=1.4) +
  annotate("text", x=ate_est+0.02, y=Inf, label=glue("ATE = {round(ate_est,3)}"),
           vjust=2, hjust=0, color=HF_ORANGE, fontface="bold", size=4) +
  labs(title="CATE Distribution — Dietary Diversity",
       subtitle=glue("BLP p = {round(blp_p,3)} — no significant heterogeneity"),
       x="Estimated CATE", y="Count") +
  theme_minimal(base_size=13) +
  theme(plot.title=element_text(face="bold",color=HF_BLUE),
        plot.subtitle=element_text(color=HF_GRAY))

vi <- variable_importance(cf)
vi_df <- tibble(variable=cate_vars, importance=as.numeric(vi)) |>
  arrange(desc(importance)) |> slice_head(n=8) |>
  mutate(variable=fct_reorder(variable, importance))

p2 <- ggplot(vi_df, aes(x=importance, y=variable)) +
  geom_col(fill=HF_BLUE, alpha=0.85) +
  labs(title="Variable Importance", x="Importance", y=NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size=13) +
  theme(plot.title=element_text(face="bold",color=HF_BLUE))

p1 + p2
```

::: {.key-finding}
**Equity finding:** Across 12 outcomes screened (7 domain indices, 3 factor scores, 2 primary), only caregiver mental health (DASS) shows marginally detectable heterogeneity (BLP p = 0.07). All child nutrition outcomes show **no heterogeneity** — the program benefits all families similarly. This is good news for scale-up: **universal delivery works**, and HF 2.0 should personalize *content* (which topics to emphasize), not *who to treat*.
:::

---

## A4 — Heterogeneity Screening: Domain Summary Indices

```{r het-screening}
#| fig-height: 2.5
#| fig-width: 10

library(kableExtra)

screening_data <- tibble(
  Index = c("Mental Health", "Caregiver Support", "Child Nutrition"),
  Components = c("EPDS + DASS", "SS + Knowledge", "Child Health + CF + BFI"),
  N = c(816L, 956L, 938L),
  ATE = c("—", "—", "—"),
  `BLP p` = c("—", "—", "—"),
  `RATE` = c("—", "—", "—"),
  `RATE p` = c("—", "—", "—")
)

# Note: actual values populated at render time from 03_domain_indices.R output
# Using placeholder structure — values shown in rendered A3 causal forest above
screening_data |>
  kable(align = "llccccc") |>
  kable_styling(font_size = 14, bootstrap_options = c("striped")) |>
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, background = HF_BLUE, color = "white") |>
  footnote(general = "Anderson (2008) ICW indices · Causal forest: 4,000 trees, tuned · See hf_heterogeneity.html for full results")
```

::: {.policy}
**For technical questions:** Full results with 4-panel figures (CATE distribution, variable importance, GATES, TOC curves) available in the companion analysis notebook: `hf_heterogeneity.html`
:::

---

## A5 — From Heterogeneity to Equity: Two Studies

::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}

<div style="font-size: 1.0em; font-weight: 600; color: #1f4e79; margin-bottom: 0.5em;">Sylvia et al. (2021) — Parenting Intervention</div>

![CATE density: bimodal distribution showing clear treatment effect heterogeneity](../images/QQ-CATE-1.png){width="90%"}

::: {.key-finding style="font-size: 0.85em;"}
**Significant heterogeneity detected.** Disadvantaged children (low baseline parental investment) benefited **+0.46 SD more** than others. Top predictor: baseline parental investment (27% importance).
:::

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

<div style="font-size: 1.0em; font-weight: 600; color: #4a7c59; margin-bottom: 0.5em;">Healthy Future 1.0 — This Study</div>

```{r cate-contrast}
#| fig-height: 3
#| fig-width: 4.5

# Load CATE results from the comprehensive screening
cate_res <- readRDS(here::here("data", "cate_results.rds"))
dds_cates <- cate_res[["FLVAR_FEED_DDS"]]$cates
ate_dds   <- cate_res[["FLVAR_FEED_DDS"]]$ate_est

ggplot(tibble(cate = dds_cates), aes(x = cate)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 25,
                 fill = HF_GREEN, color = "white", alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_density(color = "#2c5f3a", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = ate_dds, color = HF_ORANGE, linewidth = 1.2) +
  labs(title = "CATE Density — Dietary Diversity",
       subtitle = "Compressed: all families benefit similarly",
       x = "Estimated CATE", y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", color = "#2c5f3a"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(color = HF_GRAY, size = 9))
```

::: {.policy style="font-size: 0.85em;"}
**No significant heterogeneity** across 12 outcomes screened. The program benefits **all families similarly** — an equity finding. Program design, not family characteristics, drives impact.
:::

:::
:::

::: {.key-finding style="margin-top: 0.5em; text-align: center; font-size: 0.95em;"}
*The structured, app-guided delivery in Healthy Future equalizes outcomes across family types — a design feature, not a limitation. HF 2.0 should personalize content, not targeting.*
:::
